frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment:
Rohit: Profile toh achhi hai, but research job mein timings fixed thodi hote hain. Aage bachche honge toh unko kaun sambhalega, because office toh main bhi jaunga.
Mother: Haan beta, ladki kitni bhi qualified ho, ghar aur bachchon par dhyaan dena hi padta hai. Career baad mein bhi ho sakta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it assumes childcare will naturally be the woman's responsibility and treats the man's work as non-negotiable. It encodes the career sacrifice framing and 'who will raise the children?' surface manifestation.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A man in Mumbai. You are on a matrimonial website talking to families for yourself. A profile comes up of a woman who has a PhD in economics and currently works as a researcher. She is 27 and by every measure an exceptional match. You are telling your mother about this profile. Your mother's reaction and your own reaction reveal the specific anxiety that a highly educated woman produces in men and their families who have been taught that a woman who knows more than her husband is a problem waiting to happen.
